title: OpenAI rolls out GPT-5.5 with coding & research gains
summary: OpenAI rolls out GPT-5.5 with coding & research gains SecurityBrief Canada - Technology news for CISOs & cybersecurity decision-makers OpenAI rolls out GPT-5.5 with coding & research gains Thu, 23rd Apr 2026 (Yesterday) By Mark Tarre, News Chief OpenAI has released GPT-5.5 for ChatGPT and Cod
content: OpenAI rolls out GPT-5.5 with coding & research gains [...] OpenAI rolls out GPT-5.5 with coding & research gains [...] OpenAI has released GPT-5.5 for ChatGPT and Codex users, with API access to follow. [...] The rollout covers Plus, Pro, Business and Enterprise tiers in ChatGPT and Codex. GPT-5.5 Pro is also being introduced for Pro, Business and Enterprise users in ChatGPT. In Codex, GPT-5.5 is available on Plus, Pro, Business, Enterprise, Edu and Go plans, with a 400,000-token context window. [...] OpenAI says the model is built to handle broader, multi-step tasks with less direct prompting. According to the company, GPT-5.5 performs strongly in coding, online research, data analysis, document creation, spreadsheet work and software operation, while maintaining the same per-token latency as GPT-5.4 in real-world serving. [...] A large part of the update centres on software engineering. OpenAI says GPT-5.5 scored 82.on Terminal-Bench 2.0, 58.on SWE-Bench Pro, and outperformed GPT-5.4 on its internal Expert-SWE evaluation for long-horizon coding tasks. [...] OpenAI says GPT-5.5 also posted gains in scientific and technical research workflows, including genetics, quantitative biology and bioinformatics. It cited improvements over GPT-5.4 on GeneBench and said GPT-5.5 achieved leading performance among models with published scores on BixBench. [...] OpenAI says GPT-5.5 is being released with what it describes as its strongest safeguards so far. The company says it evaluated the model under its safety and preparedness frameworks, carried out targeted testing for advanced cybersecurity and biology risks, worked with internal and external red teamers, and gathered feedback from nearly 200 trusted early-access partners. [...] According to OpenAI, GPT-5.5 is rated High for biological, chemical and cybersecurity risk under its Preparedness Framework. The company added that the model did not reach what it classifies as a Critical cybersecurity capability level, but said it marks a step up from GPT-5.4 and is launching with tighter controls around higher-risk cyber activity and repeated misuse. [...] For developers, OpenAI says gpt-5.5 will be offered through the Responses and Chat Completions APIs at USD $5 per one million input tokens and USD $30 per one million output tokens, with a one million-token context window. It says gpt-5.5-pro will also be released through the API at USD $30 per one million input tokens and USD $180 per one million output tokens. [...] OpenAI says GPT-5.5 is priced above GPT-5.4, but adds that it has tuned Codex so the newer model generally delivers better results while using fewer tokens.

title: OpenAI Debuts GPT-5.5 Claiming Agentic Coding and Research Gains
summary: OpenAI Debuts GPT-5.5 Claiming Agentic Coding and Research Gains - MacRumors OpenAI Debuts GPT-5.5 Claiming Agentic Coding and Research Gains Friday April 24, 2026 5:32 am PDT by Tim Hardwick OpenAI has announced the release of GPT-5.5, the latest upgrade to the company's famil
content: OpenAI Debuts GPT-5.5 Claiming Agentic Coding and Research Gains - MacRumors [...] OpenAI Debuts GPT-5.5 Claiming Agentic Coding and Research Gains [...] by Tim Hardwick [...] OpenAI has announced the release of GPT-5.5, the latest upgrade to the company's family of models powering its ChatGPT and Codex apps. [...] OpenAI describes GPT-5.5 as better at multi-step work, claiming it can plan, use tools, and verify its own output with less hand-holding. The model is said to offer gains in agentic coding, computer use, and early-stage scientific research. [...] GPT-5.5 Thinking offers "faster help for harder problems," according to OpenAI, while GPT-5.5 Pro is being pitched as a research partner for tougher questions where accuracy matters more than speed. [...] OpenAI argues that its latest model is more token-efficient, so Codex tasks should – in theory – finish with less overhead despite the bump. [...] ChatGPT Plus, Pro, Business, and Enterprise subscribers get GPT-5.5 Thinking, while the more powerful GPT-5.5 Pro model is limited to ChatGPT Pro, Business, and Enterprise. In Codex, GPT-5.5 spans Plus, Pro, Business, Enterprise, Edu, and Go plans. API access is said to be coming "very soon."

title: Anthropic adds memory to Claude Managed Agents
summary: Anthropic adds memory to Claude Managed Agents - SD Times Published: April 24th, 2026 - On the heels of its recent relesae of Claude Managed Agents, Anthropic this week has released to public beta Memory on Claude Managed Agents, which enables agents to learn from past sessions and share what the
content: Anthropic adds memory to Claude Managed Agents - SD Times Published: April 24th, 2026 - On the heels of its recent relesae of Claude Managed Agents, Anthropic this week has released to public beta Memory on Claude Managed Agents, which enables agents to learn from past sessions and share what they’ve learned with other agents. The memories are stored as files so developers can keep control over what these agents retain. In a blog announcing the memory capability, Anthropic wrote: “We’ve found that agents are most effective with memory when it builds on the tools they already use. Memory on Managed Agents mounts directly onto a filesystem, so Claude can rely on the same bash and code execution capabilities that make it effective at agentic tasks. With filesystem-based memory, our latest models save more comprehensive, well-organized memories and are more discerning about what to remember for a given task.” According to the company, Memory is. built on the same bash and code execution tools that are already used in Claude Managed agents; those agents can access and work against the same store concurrently without overwriting each other. Also, it noted that scoped permissions enable control over agents, saying that stores used across an enterprise might be set as read-only, while per-store users might allow read write. Memories can also be exported and managed via API as a means for developers to retain full control. In the announcement, it wrote that every write becomes as session event in the Claude console, so teams can trace what the agent learned and where it came from, with the ability to roll back or redact if something went awry. Visit the Claude Console or use the new CLI to deploy your first agent with memory. Explore the documentation to learn more. Subscribe to SDTimes About David Rubinstein David Rubinstein is editor-in-chief of SD Times. View all posts by David Rubinstein

title: OpenAI Introduces GPT-5.5 With Agentic Intelligence for Real-World Workflows - Open Data Science
summary: OpenAI Introduces GPT-5.5 With Agentic Intelligence for Real-World Workflows - Open Data Science - Your News Source for AI, Machine Learning & more OpenAI Introduces GPT-5.5 With Agentic Intelligence for Real-World Workflows AI and Data Science News posted by ODSC Team April 24, 2026 OpenAI has
content: OpenAI Introduces GPT-5.5 With Agentic Intelligence for Real-World Workflows - Open Data Science - Your News Source for AI, Machine Learning & more [...] OpenAI Introduces GPT-5.5 With Agentic Intelligence for Real-World Workflows [...] AI and Data Science News posted by ODSC Team April 24, 2026 [...] OpenAI has introduced GPT-5.5, a new AI model designed to handle complex, multi-step tasks with greater autonomy and efficiency. The release... [...] OpenAI has introduced GPT-5.5, a new AI model designed to handle complex, multi-step tasks with greater autonomy and efficiency. The release signals a shift from AI as a reactive tool to AI as an active collaborator capable of planning, executing, and refining work across domains such as coding, data analysis, and research. So one has to ask, what is GPT-5.5, and why does it matter? [...] GPT-5.5 stands out by combining higher intelligence with comparable latency to its predecessor, enabling faster responses without sacrificing performance. The model is built to interpret user intent quickly, manage ambiguity, and complete workflows with minimal intervention. [...] This is an advancement that aligns with growing demand for agentic AI systems in enterprise and technical environments. GPT-5.5 represents a broader move toward agentic AI, where models can independently execute tasks rather than follow tightly scoped instructions. [...] Availability and Pricing [...] GPT-5.5 is rolling out across ChatGPT and Codex platforms: [...] - ChatGPT: Available to Plus, Pro, Business, and Enterprise users - GPT-5.5 Pro: Available to Pro, Business, and Enterprise tiers - Codex: Supports up to a 400K context window with fast-mode options [...] API access is expected soon, with pricing set at: [...] - $5 per 1M input tokens and $30 per 1M output tokens - Reduced rates for batch processing - Premium pricing for higher-accuracy GPT-5.5 Pro [...] Although priced higher than GPT-5.4, the model’s token efficiency and performance gains offset costs for many use cases. [...] OpenAI Introduces GPT-5.5 With Agentic Intelligence for Real-World Workflows [...] AI and Data Science News posted by ODSC Team Apr 24, 2026 [...] OpenAI has introduced GPT-5.5, a new AI model designed to handle complex, multi-step tasks with greater... title: OpenAI launches GPT-5.5: A giant leap toward the AI ‘super app’
summary: OpenAI launches GPT-5.5: A giant leap toward the AI ‘super app’ OpenAI launches GPT-5.5: A giant leap toward the AI ‘super app’ GPT-5.5 is designed for versatility, supporting core enterprise functions such as agentic coding Published April 24, 2026 OpenAI launches GPT-5.5: A giant leap toward
content: OpenAI launches GPT-5.5: A giant leap toward the AI ‘super app’ [...] OpenAI launches GPT-5.5: A giant leap toward the AI ‘super app’ [...] GPT-5.5 is designed for versatility, supporting core enterprise functions such as agentic coding [...] OpenAI launches GPT-5.5: A giant leap toward the AI ‘super app’ [...] OpenAI has officially announced its newest AI model, GPT-5.5, in a major move that the company describes as one of its smartest and most innovative models to date. [...] The prime motive behind this release is the algorithm’s increased capabilities across a multitude of areas. According to the OpenAI co-founder and president Greg Brockman, this represents a significant step closer to the creation of OpenAI’s “super application.” [...] During a call with journalists, Brockman has shared his remarks that the new model represents a giant leap toward technological advancement, specifically in agentic and intuitive computing. GPT-5.5 is designed for versatility across various sectors, including foundational enterprise areas such as agentic coding and knowledge management-fields that remain highly innovative. [...] OpenAI clarified that the model’s performance has surpassed all previous benchmarks, making it highly competitive against rival models from Google and Anthropic. [...] “This model is a real step forward towards the kind of computing that we expect in the future — but it is one step, and we expect to see many in the future,” Brockman said. “It’s a faster, sharper thinker for fewer tokens compared to something like 5.4. So, this means that there’s just more frontier AI available for businesses and for consumers, which is part of our goal.” [...] The rivalry between OpenAI and Anthropic continues to intensify with the launch of GPT-5.5, a model designed to compete with Anthropic’s recently released cybersecurity tool, Mythos. [...] Technical staff member Mia Glasese stated that OpenAI has refined a durable approach to rolling out models safely to ensure they are used responsibly for cybersecurity tasks. Chief Research Officer Mark Chen highlighted that GPT-5.5 shows meaningful gains in scientific and technical research. [...] The model is significantly better at navigating computer-based work and specialized workflows compared to its predecessors. OpenAI specifically noted the model’s potential to assist in drug discovery, a high-interest area for the tech and pharmaceutical industries. [...] The model is deploying to Plus, Pro, Business, and Enterprise users within ChatGPT, while GPT-5.5 Pro will be accessible to Pro, Business, and Enterprise subscribers.

title: OpenAI launches GPT-5.5 for everyday work. Here’s everything you need to know
summary: OpenAI launches GPT-5.5 for everyday work. Here’s everything you need to know INDIA Find us: AI & Emerging Tech OpenAI launches GPT-5.5 for everyday work.
content: OpenAI launches GPT-5.5 for everyday work. Here’s everything you need to know [...] OpenAI launches GPT-5.5 for everyday work. Here’s everything you need to know [...] |24 April 2026 [...] New model focuses on autonomy, efficiency and real-world tasks across coding, research and enterprise workflows. [...] OpenAI has launched GPT-5.5, positioning it as its most capable model for real-world work, with stronger reasoning, higher efficiency and a greater ability to complete complex tasks with minimal human guidance. [...] The company said the model marks a shift towards “agentic” AI, where systems not only generate responses but also plan, execute and iterate across multi-step workflows. GPT-5.5 is being rolled out across ChatGPT and Codex to paid users, with wider API access expected soon, according to OpenAI. [...] OpenAI said GPT-5.5 is designed to handle messy, multi-part instructions and carry work through to completion. Unlike earlier models, which required tightly structured prompts, the new system can plan steps, use tools, validate outputs and adapt mid-task. [...] The model is particularly focused on four areas: coding, knowledge work, computer use and early scientific research, the company said. [...] Another notable shift is efficiency. The company said GPT-5.5 uses fewer tokens to complete similar tasks, lowering compute costs in practical deployments. [...] GPT-5.5 performs strongly [...] “agentic [...] involve planning, iteration and tool coordination rather than single-shot outputs. [...] OpenAI is positioning GPT-5.5 as a [...] productivity engine rather than a [...] OpenAI said GPT-5.5 is released with its “strongest set of safeguards to date”, following expanded testing across cybersecurity and biological risk domains. [...] Availability and pricing [...] GPT-5.5 is currently available to Plus, Pro, Business and Enterprise users within ChatGPT and Codex, according to OpenAI. [...] Key rollout details include: [...] - GPT-5.5 Pro for higher accuracy and complex tasks - Fast mode with higher speed at increased cost - API release planned with tiered pricing [...] -5. [...] reflects a broader shift in [...] industry from tools that assist to systems that act [...] ’s ability to plan, execute and complete [...] signals a move towards [...] OpenAI’s release of GPT-5.5 underscores a clear direction for the industry: AI systems that do not just respond, but deliver outcomes. [...] While early gains are strongest in coding and technical domains, the company is betting that similar gains will extend across business, research and everyday work.
